title: Internationalization Engineering
subtitle: Multi-language Support Engineering
description: AI Agents in global deployments require multi-language interaction support, localized prompts, and cross-language context management. Internationalization engineering involves language detection, dynamic translation, cultural adaptation, and coordination of multi-language knowledge bases to ensure Agent consistency and accuracy across different language environments.
sub_problems: Language Detection and Routing: Automatically identify user input language and route to corresponding processing pipelines
Multi-language Prompt Management: Maintain and version control multi-language prompt templates while ensuring semantic consistency
Translation Quality Control: Handle translation errors, cultural differences, and domain terminology accuracy
Multi-language Context Fusion: Correctly maintain conversation history and knowledge retrieval in mixed-language scenarios
Localization Adaptation: Handle timezone, currency, date format, and other region-specific data representations
best_practices: Use language tags (BCP 47) for unified language identification and establish clear language routing strategies
Separate prompt templates from translated content, centrally maintain multi-language versions through configuration management systems
Introduce translation quality scoring mechanisms and conduct manual review and feedback loops for critical path translations
Explicitly mark language tags in context to avoid ambiguity and errors caused by language mixing
Establish multi-language test sets and benchmarks, regularly evaluate Agent performance differences across languages
